items.content_statement: A student can draw a picture of themselves and describe what they have drawn using the prompt 'This is me, my name is and I am...' saying as much as they can
items.classification_rationale: This describes a specific procedural skill with clear performance criteria that can be assessed through direct observation and rubric

items.content_statement: Can say five things that they like about themselves
items.classification_rationale: This is a procedural skill with clear performance criteria - the learner must be able to articulate specific positive self-attributes when prompted. It's occasion-triggered (when asked) and has measurable criteria (five things).

items.content_statement: Can say three things that are important to them
items.classification_rationale: This describes a specific procedural capability - articulating personal values/priorities when prompted. It's a discrete skill with clear performance criteria (can state three specific things), not a sustained orientation or analytical reasoning across multiple lenses.

items.content_statement: Can talk about what happens in their bodies when they feel happy, angry and scared
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to reflect on emotional experiences - reasoning about internal bodily states across different emotional contexts

items.content_statement: Listens to different pieces of music and can say what it makes them feel
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to listen and reflect - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about internal emotional responses

items.content_statement: Uses regular breathing as a calming strategy
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation - the default use of breathing as a calming strategy across contexts, which exists only as enacted over time

items.content_statement: Uses at least twelve different words for emotion in describing: a play situation; losing something; a day at home
items.classification_rationale: This describes a specific procedural skill with clear performance criteria (twelve different emotion words) applied to defined contexts. It is occasion-triggered (when asked to describe situations) and has measurable standards, making it Type 1 procedural knowledge.

items.content_statement: Can say what cheers them up when they are fed up
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires self-reflection and articulation of personal coping strategies

items.content_statement: Can say three things that are the same and three things that are different about themselves and another person
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when prompted to compare self and others - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about similarities and differences

items.content_statement: Joins in a discussion about how this class can make sure everyone feels they belong here
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when discussion situations arise - reasoning about belonging and inclusion through multiple perspectives

items.content_statement: Can say 'personal positives' about classmates
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when occasions call for identifying and articulating positive qualities in peers - requires reasoning about character traits and appropriate expression

items.content_statement: Demonstrates the ability to share
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that exists only as it is enacted over time across contexts - sharing as a default behavior pattern rather than an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: In a pair share, can identify three qualities or actions that would make someone a good friend
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires reasoning about friendship qualities through multiple possible frameworks, no single correct answer but better and worse analyses

items.content_statement: Can say how a teddy might feel if he: was left out; saw someone fall off a bike; had a new baby sister
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted with specific scenarios - requires reasoning about emotional responses across different situations

items.content_statement: Can recognize non-verbal expression of basic emotions — happy, surprised, scared, sad and worried.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for emotion recognition; involves interpreting behavioral cues through emotional understanding framework

items.content_statement: Can say three things they want to achieve this week
items.classification_rationale: This describes a specific procedural capability with clear criteria (saying three specific things) that can be directly assessed through performance

items.content_statement: Invites others to join in games
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained social orientation that operates by default across contexts - the inclination to include others in play activities. This is an enacted capacity that exists only as it is demonstrated over time in real social situations.

items.content_statement: Is willing to try new things
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - willingness to try new things operates as an ongoing disposition across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: In a paired discussion, identifies a situation where someone is laughing at someone and how this might feel, and when someone is laughing with someone and how this might feel
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - distinguishing between social situations and reasoning about emotional impacts through empathy lens

items.content_statement: Engages with a class project on making others feel good (filling buckets)
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation toward making others feel good, enacted through engagement with projects - this is a default orientation that exists only as it is enacted over time

items.content_statement: Clearly, firmly and politely states what they want in conflict situations
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical/communication skill deployed when conflict situations call for assertive response; quality of execution varies with reasoning about context and approach

items.content_statement: Knows when something is fair or not
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about fairness criteria - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or application to scenarios

items.content_statement: how to stand up for someone
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered capability deployed when a situation calls for advocacy - analytical skill requiring reasoning about context and appropriate response
prerequisite_lts: Knows when something is fair or not

items.content_statement: Checks whether a hurt was an accident or deliberate
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - evaluating intent behind harmful actions through reasoning about evidence and context

items.content_statement: Can talk about things that are 'inside' hurts and those that are 'outside' hurts
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - distinguishing between internal and external aspects of emotional hurt requires reasoning and interpretive framework

items.content_statement: Can say how a lie can grow and get out of control and give three reasons why it might be better to tell the truth
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about consequences and reasoning - factual understanding that can be explained and assessed through clear criteria

items.content_statement: Within a small group, identifies the responsibilities that go with five rights or freedoms.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - identifying connections between rights and responsibilities requires reasoning across multiple frameworks and contexts, with quality of analysis being the key assessment criterion

items.content_statement: Grows a flower from a seed
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill with clear steps and observable outcome - Type 1 procedural

items.content_statement: Talks in a pair share about what happened: Did the flower grow by itself? What helped it grow? How much was nature and how much was what you did to help?
items.classification_rationale: Analytical reasoning about multiple factors and causation - occasion-triggered when prompted to reflect - Type 2
prerequisite_lts: Grows a flower from a seed

items.content_statement: Identifies three things that would make someone happy for a short time and three things that would make someone happy all or most of the time
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability that requires reasoning about different types of happiness and their duration - multiple valid frameworks could apply (psychological, philosophical, personal values), with quality of reasoning determining assessment outcome

items.content_statement: A student can draw three pictures of themselves in different roles and can talk about the different ways they behave in each of these roles
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability - reflecting on and articulating how one's behavior varies across different social contexts. This is occasion-prompted self-reflection skill that can be deployed when asked to analyze one's social roles.

items.content_statement: Can identify three things they are good at, three qualities they have and three strengths they think they might need in the future
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when prompted for self-reflection - reasoning about personal strengths across current and future contexts. No hard prerequisite sequence, but quality of self-analysis can be assessed.

items.content_statement: Can say three things that are important to them and why they are important
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability - reflecting on personal values and articulating reasoning about importance. This is occasion-prompted (when asked to reflect) and involves reasoning quality about what matters and why.

items.content_statement: Knows about the continuum of emotions
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how emotions exist on a continuum - factual content to be held

items.content_statement: Can take part in a discussion about when you might be in control of an emotion and when it controls you and say what makes a difference
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when discussion situations call for it - reasoning about emotional control across multiple scenarios
prerequisite_lts: Knows about the continuum of emotions

items.content_statement: Can take part in a discussion about how the arts (music/dance and drama) can impact on how someone feels
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a discussion situation calls for it - reasoning about arts impact on emotions through multiple lenses

items.content_statement: Can identify four different ways to deal with anger and decide which is the preferred option and why
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when anger situations arise - requires reasoning across multiple options and justifying preference with rationale

items.content_statement: Can identify at least five words showing different aspects of the following emotions: happiness; sadness; interest; anger.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge - vocabulary items that must be learned and can be assessed through knowledge testing. There is a clear correct/incorrect distinction for emotion vocabulary.

items.content_statement: Knows how thinking can affect how you feel and says what thinking strategies are useful
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the relationship between thinking and feeling, plus factual knowledge of specific strategies - hard prerequisite sequence content

items.content_statement: Can write about ways in which everyone is unique and the reasons it is valuable
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to write about uniqueness and value - requires reasoning across multiple frameworks about human diversity and worth

items.content_statement: Can say what 'The Golden Rule' is and how it appears in all major religions
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about a specific concept and its manifestation across religions - factual content that can be known and recalled

items.content_statement: Can say what 'The Golden Rule' means in practice
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of how a principle translates to practical application - understanding that builds on knowing what the Golden Rule is
prerequisite_lts: Can say what 'The Golden Rule' is and how it appears in all major religions

items.content_statement: Demonstrates the 'rules' of good conversation — a balance between talking and listening
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation that exists only as enacted across conversational contexts over time; requires observation of actual conversational behaviour

items.content_statement: Demonstrates regular small acts of kindness — goes out of their way to help others
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation that operates across contexts and exists only as enacted over time

items.content_statement: Works well within a group to achieve a given target
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across group contexts - collaborative working capacity that exists only as enacted over time

items.content_statement: Shows an understanding of what someone might feel if they: were bullied; lost something or someone; broke a leg; failed a test; were hungry or tired
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for perspective-taking - reasoning about others' emotional states across multiple scenarios with varying quality of analysis possible

items.content_statement: Can view a film and identify the feelings of characters within a situation by their voice tone, body posture and facial expression
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when viewing films - interpreting emotional cues through multiple observable indicators requires reasoning quality assessment

items.content_statement: Demonstrates an interest in getting better at something
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - interest in improvement operates as an ongoing disposition across contexts, not triggered by specific occasions

items.content_statement: Understands the concept of personal bests
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - conceptual understanding of what personal bests means

items.content_statement: Is aware of those who have been left out and includes them.
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - the capacity to notice exclusion and act inclusively operates as an ongoing disposition across contexts, not just when prompted by specific situations

items.content_statement: Accepts mistakes as part of learning
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation toward mistakes that operates across contexts over time, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Identifies three things to be grateful for and three things to look forward to
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires reflection and evaluation across multiple personal contexts to identify meaningful items

items.content_statement: Can identify one situation in which positive thinking helped and can talk/write about the difference it made
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to reflect on and articulate the impact of positive thinking - requires reasoning about cause-effect relationships and quality of analysis matters

items.content_statement: Stands up for others who are less able to stand up for themselves
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained default orientation - a quality that exists only as it is enacted over time across contexts, requiring observation of real behaviour for valid evidence

items.content_statement: Knows when a situation is not safe and leaves
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - requires reasoning about safety indicators across multiple contexts with no single correct framework

items.content_statement: Knows about responsibility pie-charts: how much was down to me, how much to someone else, how much to chance
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about a specific analytical tool - responsibility pie-charts and their components. This is factual content the learner must hold.

items.content_statement: Offers to make up for harm caused
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - the tendency to offer repair when harm has been caused operates as an ongoing dispositional capacity across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: In a small group, talks about why people don't always get what they deserve, and what this means for how we see the world and others in it
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a discussion situation calls for it - reasoning about fairness, justice, and worldview implications through multiple possible lenses

items.content_statement: In small groups, using the Strengths in Circles cards on inclusion and equity, chooses one from each set and decides how this will be put into practice in the classroom
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when a specific task situation calls for it - selecting from options and reasoning about practical implementation requires multiple valid approaches and reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: Chooses a symbol or picture that illustrates what really matters in life and talks about the choice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires reasoning about values and meaning to select appropriate symbols and articulate rationale

items.content_statement: Identifies someone who is a role model for how to live life well and researches what that person did
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires evaluating what constitutes 'living life well' and applying criteria to identify suitable role models, then conducting research

items.content_statement: Has reflected on their developing identity
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation - reflection on identity is an ongoing default practice, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Writes about a role model and why they have chosen this person
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability - selecting and justifying choice of role model requires reasoning across multiple considerations when prompted

items.content_statement: Can talk about how they have developed up to five character strengths and what strengths they are working on and why these are valuable
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to reflect on and articulate personal development - requires reasoning about personal growth and value justification

items.content_statement: Can identify the five values and three beliefs about the world that impact most in how they choose to live their lives
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for self-reflection - identifying and articulating personal values and beliefs that guide life choices. This is occasion-triggered reasoning skill, not sustained orientation.

items.content_statement: Knows the role of the amygdala and how it can hijack 'thinking'
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about brain function - factual content that must be held

items.content_statement: Can give an example of when the amygdala might hijack thinking
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill - applying factual knowledge to generate examples on demand
prerequisite_lts: Knows the role of the amygdala and how it can hijack 'thinking'

items.content_statement: Knows about 'mirroring' neurons
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about brain function - factual content that must be held

items.content_statement: Can give an example of when mirroring neurons might operate
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill - applying factual knowledge to generate examples on demand

items.content_statement: Watches a film and writes a review about the characters, what happened to them and the emotional content. What did it make them feel and why?
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - analyzing emotional content through multiple lenses (character analysis, personal emotional response, causal reasoning about emotional impact). No single correct answer but better and worse analyses.

items.content_statement: Can list five ways in which someone might change how they feel and write the pros and cons of each
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about emotion regulation strategies and their trade-offs - factual content that can be listed and explained with clear right/wrong answers

items.content_statement: Can reflect on a learning task and describe a range of emotions that were experienced in engagement, challenges and completion
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to reflect on learning experiences - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about emotional experiences

items.content_statement: Can identify ways in which friends can support resilience and ways in which this can be undermined
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when examining friendship dynamics - requires reasoning across multiple factors to identify supportive vs undermining behaviours

items.content_statement: Can give five reasons why 'shared humanity' matters for the future of our world
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - specific factual content about reasons that can be recalled and stated

items.content_statement: With a partner makes a poster on gender, sexuality or disability to prevent discrimination
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a specific task calls for it - creating educational materials requires reasoning about discrimination prevention through multiple lenses

items.content_statement: Demonstrates the skills of 'active listening', paying verbal and non-verbal attention, responding verbally and non-verbally, asking questions, clarifying meaning
items.classification_rationale: Analytical/reasoning capability deployed when interpersonal situations call for it - active listening skills are occasion-triggered rather than sustained default orientation

items.content_statement: Knows what respect means, how it is demonstrated and why it is important for a healthy relationship
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the definition, manifestations, and importance of respect - factual/conceptual content that can be assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: Can initiate and maintain a conversation with someone who is previously unknown
items.classification_rationale: Analytical/reasoning capability deployed when a social situation calls for it - occasion-triggered skill requiring judgment about how to approach and sustain interaction with strangers

items.content_statement: Can say when it might be a good time to approach a parent with a request and when best to wait, and identify factors that influence the decision.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - reasoning about timing and contextual factors in interpersonal situations

items.content_statement: Recognizes that everyone has their own story and does not make quick judgements
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained default orientation - a way of consistently approaching others across contexts rather than a skill deployed when prompted. The capacity to withhold quick judgement and recognize others' complexity operates as an ongoing stance.

items.content_statement: Can divide a task into a series of between 8 and 12 achievable steps
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a task requires breakdown - occasion-triggered skill with reasoning quality determining success

items.content_statement: Can discuss risk taking and look at how to assess risk against potential outcomes
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for risk assessment - multiple valid frameworks for evaluating risk, reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: Gives help to others so that they can do well at something
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation of helping others succeed - this is an enacted capacity that operates across contexts and exists only as it is demonstrated over time

items.content_statement: Keeps a daily gratitude journal for a month: writing in three different blessings, one for the day, one for life and one for anything else
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained practice enacted over time (daily for a month) - a dispositional orientation toward gratitude that exists only as it is enacted across contexts

items.content_statement: Can take five 'problems' and give a 'solution' for each: what you are aiming for rather than what you want to get rid of
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when presented with problems - reframing problems into solution-focused goals requires reasoning across multiple perspectives and can be done with varying quality

items.content_statement: Identifies the short- and long-term outcomes of overt aggression, passive aggression, timidity and appropriate assertiveness
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - identifying and evaluating outcomes across multiple behavioral frameworks requires reasoning quality assessment

items.content_statement: Checks on intention before jumping to a negative conclusion in a potential conflict
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - the habitual practice of checking intention rather than assuming negative intent when conflicts arise. This is an enacted capacity that operates by default across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill.

items.content_statement: Can list ways to say how something could have been done differently
items.classification_rationale: This describes a procedural skill with clear performance criteria - the ability to generate and articulate alternative approaches. This is a Type 1 procedural capability that can be assessed against clear criteria for completeness and appropriateness of suggestions.

items.content_statement: Responds positively to an attempt to repair harm caused
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - how one characteristically responds to repair attempts across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Identifies three factors that influence how someone might vote in an election
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to examine voting behaviour - multiple valid frameworks exist for identifying influences (economic, social, personal values, etc.), quality of reasoning determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Takes part in a project to protect the environment
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation toward environmental action that exists only as enacted behavior over time - requires observation of actual participation across contexts

items.content_statement: Joins in a discussion about what it means to live 'a good life'
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when discussion situations arise - reasoning about meaning and values through multiple possible frameworks

items.content_statement: Makes a photo portfolio of what is meaningful and writes briefly why each photo is included
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to reflect on meaning - requires reasoning about personal significance and articulating justifications

items.content_statement: Can write a page about themselves, who they are, what they believe and the person they hope to become in the future
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability - synthesizing self-knowledge across multiple dimensions (identity, values, aspirations) into coherent written expression. This is occasion-prompted reasoning that can be assessed through quality of analysis and reflection.

items.content_statement: Can identify five character strengths and also the shadow sides of strengths
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about character strengths and their shadow sides - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge test

items.content_statement: Can say what strengths have been of most value to them in their life
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability requiring reflection and evaluation of personal experience - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome
prerequisite_lts: Can identify five character strengths and also the shadow sides of strengths

items.content_statement: Are clear about a range of values and the reasons they have these
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained orientation - being clear about one's values operates as a default state across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Can tell the difference between being sad and being depressed
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about definitional distinctions between emotional states - hard prerequisite sequence applies

items.content_statement: Knows who to seek out for support when they are struggling
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about support resources and appropriate help-seeking pathways

items.content_statement: Can write a page about an historical event in which people's emotions were manipulated and how this happened
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - requires reasoning across multiple lenses (historical analysis, emotional manipulation dynamics, causal explanation) with quality of reasoning determining assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Is using one physical strategy, one social strategy and one cognitive strategy on a regular basis to help stay resilient
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - regular use of strategies across contexts over time, not occasion-triggered deployment

items.content_statement: Joins in a discussion about a current controversial issue or a film, talking about how people might feel about this
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when discussion situations arise - reasoning about others' emotional responses through multiple perspectives

items.content_statement: Has considered the usefulness of mindfulness or meditation as a resilience strategy
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about the utility of specific strategies - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or reasoning about applications

items.content_statement: Can identify times where people have been dehumanized, what contributes to this and what has happened to them.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when examining historical or contemporary situations - requires reasoning across multiple contexts and lenses to identify dehumanization patterns, contributing factors, and consequences

items.content_statement: Is able to identify ways in which shared humanity might be put into practice from the individual to the community to the social/political level.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - identifying applications across multiple contexts/levels requires reasoning quality assessment

items.content_statement: Is effective within a group discussion. Includes others and builds on their ideas.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical or reasoning capability deployed when group discussion situations arise - occasion-triggered skill requiring judgment about effectiveness, inclusion strategies, and building on ideas

items.content_statement: Knows what trust means, how it is demonstrated and why it is important for a healthy relationship
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the definition, demonstration, and importance of trust - factual/conceptual content that can be assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: Works with a group to take a project from initiation to completion
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a collaborative project situation arises - Type 2 occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Can write about the factors that promoted collaboration between team members
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability to evaluate and articulate collaboration factors - Type 2 reasoning skill

items.content_statement: Can assess the emotional content of a situation and say what might be said or done to make this worse or better. This can be from a book or film.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - assessing emotional content and reasoning about interventions requires multiple interpretive frameworks and reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: Can write a story from the different perspectives of the main characters
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a writing task calls for it - requires reasoning about character motivations and viewpoints across multiple perspectives

items.content_statement: Can reflect on a personal achievement and what contributed to success
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to examine past experiences - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Can say what helps resist peer pressure so you act according to your own values
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about strategies for resisting peer pressure - factual content that can be stated and assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: Can identify plans for the future, with fall-back plans if these don't happen
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when planning situations arise - requires reasoning across multiple considerations and contexts to develop quality plans and contingencies

items.content_statement: Reflects on the future, and makes a video talking about what they hope for themselves and the world in the future. Writes three things that they will do now to help this happen.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted - reflection and planning skills that involve reasoning about future possibilities and connecting aspirations to present actions

items.content_statement: Talks/writes about a life event as if it were a newspaper story, once with the headline 'Disaster' and once with the headline 'Success', identifying how things can be seen from different perspectives and we have some choice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to examine a situation - reframing events through multiple interpretive lenses to identify perspective choice

items.content_statement: With a real-life or imagined scenario, can imagine three possible resolutions and write the pros and cons of each, and give a rationale for the first choice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a conflict scenario calls for it - multiple valid approaches exist, quality of reasoning determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Checks out what two sides want in a situation and negotiates a compromise
items.classification_rationale: Analytical/reasoning capability deployed when conflict situations arise - occasion-triggered skill requiring judgment about stakeholder needs and compromise solutions

items.content_statement: Joins in a small group discussion on the meaning of altruism and whether this is a value to aim for
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a discussion situation calls for it - reasoning about values and their merit across multiple perspectives

items.content_statement: Within a small group, researches a social justice issue and devises an action plan to redress this
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - researching and planning to address social justice issues involves multiple valid approaches and reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: Joins in a small group discussion on the meaning of altruism and whether this is a value to aim for
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a discussion situation calls for it - reasoning about ethical concepts through dialogue

items.content_statement: Within a small group, researches a social justice issue and devises an action plan to redress this
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - researching and devising action plans for social justice issues requires reasoning across multiple lenses with no single correct answer

items.content_statement: Writes or speaks about the principles or values that will guide small and major decisions in the future
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to articulate guiding principles - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about values and their application to decision-making contexts

items.content_statement: Writes a personal statement setting out aspirations for being, becoming and contributing
items.classification_rationale: This describes an analytical capability deployed when the situation calls for it - writing a reflective personal statement requires reasoning across multiple frameworks about identity, values, and purpose. No single correct answer but better and worse analyses.
